options.label: Useful
options.description: Relevant and concretely helpful for downstream synthesis. A useful chunk does not need to answer the whole task alone; it is useful if it provides facts, definitions, distinctions, examples, user-style guidance, constraints, or conceptual material that can help build the final answer. Multiple chunks may be useful when they contribute different parts of the answer or different required angles of the user task.

options.label: Borderline
options.description: Related or partially helpful, but weaker than useful. Borderline includes chunks that provide background, indirect support, incomplete fragments, weaker duplicates, or context that may be kept as backup if few useful chunks exist. A chunk should be borderline rather than discarded when it contains any concrete information connected to the task.

options.label: Discarded
options.description: Clearly unrelated, empty, corrupted, misleading, severely fragmentary, or unusable for the current task. Do not discard a chunk merely because it is incomplete, stylistic, background, or only partially relevant; use borderline for those cases.
